items.headline: Senegal bans ministerial travel as oil prices double budget projections
items.body: Senegal banned government ministers from non-essential foreign travel after oil costs approached double what the government budgeted for. The move joins fuel tax cuts and electricity rationing across Africa as governments respond to the Strait of Hormuz closure.

items.headline: Sudan hospital faces critical supply shortages as violence displaces tens of thousands
items.body: Health workers at Sudan's El-Obeid Maternity Hospital report severe shortages of surgical equipment and antibiotics as violence in South Kordofan forces tens of thousands to flee. The city has come under frequent drone attacks targeting health facilities.

items.headline: Australia urges fuel conservation as Strait of Hormuz closure strains supply
items.body: Australia's prime minister urged citizens to take public transportation and avoid panic buying, warning that fuel should be saved for farmers and critical industries. While Australia imports little crude directly from the Middle East, up to 80 percent of its refined fuel comes via Asian refineries dependent on routes through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: First Chadian troops arrive in Haiti as Gang Suppression Force begins deployment
items.body: The first troops from a new UN-backed Gang Suppression Force arrived in Haiti from Chad, accompanied by the mission's special representative. The force is expected to grow to 5,500 personnel and will be empowered to make arrests, unlike the smaller Kenyan-led mission it replaces.

es.headline: Spain leads five-nation call for EU windfall tax on energy companies as fuel prices surge
es.body: Spain joined Germany, Italy, Portugal and Austria in urging the European Commission to tax war-driven energy profits. The measure would redistribute windfall gains to ease consumer burdens without adding strain to national budgets already stretched by the Iran crisis.

no.headline: Fuel tax cuts fail to lower prices as retailers raise rates hours before relief takes effect
no.body: Opposition parties forced emergency fuel tax cuts worth billions to ease pump prices driven by Middle East oil disruptions. Retailers raised prices just before the cuts took effect, leaving consumers with no relief and intensifying inflation concerns.
